sections.paragraph:
ὀλιγοχρόνιος, ον (Hdt. et al.; Polyb. 2, 35, 6; Epict. in Stob. p. 463, 1 Sch.; oft. Vett. Val., s. index; Wsd 9:5; Philo) of short duration, short-lived ἡ ἐπιδημία ἡ ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ τούτῳ τῆς σαρκὸς ταύτης μικρά ἐστιν καὶ ὀλ. 2 Cl 5:5. τὰ ἐνθάδε (w. μικρά and φθαρτά) 6:6.—DELG s.v. ὀλίγο.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Stob Stob , V a.d.—List 5

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

oft. oft. = often

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 703
